2026-06-23.title: Agentic AI: The Weapon That No Longer Needs a Warrior
2026-06-23.tldr: Every weapon begins as an extension of the hand that holds it. The spear lengthened the reach of the arm. The bow sent the point flying without the throw. The rifle placed a man's death a quarter mile beyond his sight, and the aircraft carried that death across oceans. At each turn, the distance between the warrior and the wound grew wider, and yet one thing never moved: a human chose the target

2026-06-23.title: Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
2026-06-23.tldr: Two members of the 'Scattered Spider' cybercrime group have pleaded guilty to hacking Transport for London (TfL) systems in 2024. This confirms a past breach incident targeting critical infrastructure, underscoring the ongoing threat from sophisticated groups.
2026-06-23.action: Review and strengthen security controls for critical infrastructure. Enhance monitoring for advanced persistent threats (APTs) and suspicious activities.

2026-06-23.title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
2026-06-23.tldr: The JaredFromSubway Ethereum MEV bot suffered a $15 million loss after an attacker manipulated its opportunity-detection logic. This exploit created fake cryptocurrency trading opportunities, leading to unauthorized fund transfers.
2026-06-23.action: Implement robust security audits for smart contract code and automated trading bot logic. Enforce multi-signature requirements for high-value transactions and enhance real-time monitoring for suspicious trading activities.

2026-06-23.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
2026-06-23.tldr: The "FortiBleed" campaign, linked to a Russian initial access broker, compromised Fortinet FortiGate devices to deploy custom sniffers. This allowed attackers to steal authentication secrets from network traffic.
2026-06-23.action: Immediately audit all FortiGate devices for compromise indicators, suspicious files, or unauthorized configurations. Reset all credentials that may have traversed compromised FortiGates and enhance network segmentation.

2026-06-23.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
2026-06-23.tldr: A supply chain attack has compromised ShapedPlugin's WordPress Pro plugins, injecting malicious code to create a backdoor. This allows unauthorized access and control over websites using the backdoored plugins.
2026-06-23.action: Immediately audit all WordPress installations for ShapedPlugin Pro plugins. If present, verify their integrity against trusted checksums, remove compromised versions, and update to clean versions. Implement file integrity monitoring.
2026-06-23.affected_versions: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro plugins compromised via a supply chain attack (specific vulnerable versions require vendor confirmation).
2026-06-23.how_to_determine_impact: On WordPress sites, inspect 'wp-content/plugins/' directory for 'shapedplugin-pro'. Compare file checksums with legitimate versions. Look for unexpected PHP files or modifications within plugin directories. Review web server access logs for suspicious administrative activity or web shell access.
2026-06-23.tanium_query_hint: Get File Hash['/var/www/html/wp-content/plugins/shapedplugin-pro/*'] and compare with known good hashes; Get File Exists['/var/www/html/wp-content/plugins/shapedplugin-pro/malicious-file.php'] (if specific IOCs are available); Get WordPress Version.
2026-06-23.rapid7_query_hint: Scan web servers hosting WordPress for file integrity anomalies within plugin directories. Use InsightIDR to detect web shell activity, unusual file modifications, or unauthorized admin logins on WordPress instances.

2026-06-23.title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
2026-06-23.tldr: FFmpeg has patched the 'PixelSmash' flaw, a critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution (RCE) on Jellyfin servers and denial-of-service (DoS) in applications like Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio under specific conditions.
2026-06-23.action: Update all instances of FFmpeg to the latest patched version immediately. For applications dependent on FFmpeg, ensure they are updated or confirmed to integrate the fix. Prioritize systems running media servers.
2026-06-23.affected_versions: FFmpeg prior to the patched version. Applications including Jellyfin, Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio that use vulnerable FFmpeg libraries.
2026-06-23.how_to_determine_impact: Identify the FFmpeg version installed on servers and workstations. Check media server applications (Jellyfin, Kodi, Emby, etc.) for their integrated FFmpeg version or if they've released updates addressing the flaw. Monitor for unexpected crashes or service interruptions.
2026-06-23.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications 'FFmpeg' Version; Get Running Processes 'jellyfin', 'kodi', 'emby', 'nextcloud', 'photoprism', 'obs' and identify their associated FFmpeg library versions using 'Get Module Details'.
2026-06-23.rapid7_query_hint: Scan assets for FFmpeg installations and associated media applications. Search for vulnerability IDs related to 'FFmpeg PixelSmash' once published. Review application logs for crashes or unusual behavior related to video processing.

2026-06-23.title: Eight-Year-Old Samsung KNOX Flaw Exposed Millions of Galaxy Devices to Kernel Attacks
2026-06-23.tldr: An eight-year-old critical vulnerability in Samsung KNOX has been revealed, potentially exposing millions of Galaxy devices to kernel attacks. This flaw could allow attackers to achieve elevated privileges and full device compromise.
2026-06-23.action: Ensure all Samsung Galaxy devices are updated with the very latest security patches. Implement robust Mobile Device Management (MDM) policies to enforce updates and monitor for signs of compromise, especially kernel-level anomalies.
2026-06-23.affected_versions: Millions of Samsung Galaxy devices with vulnerable versions of Samsung KNOX (specific version ranges require vendor advisories).
2026-06-23.how_to_determine_impact: Check the firmware version and KNOX version on all managed Samsung Galaxy devices. Ensure devices are running the latest security updates from Samsung. Monitor MDM logs for any signs of kernel-level exploits or unauthorized privilege escalation.
2026-06-23.tanium_query_hint: N/A (Primarily mobile device focused, not typically covered by Tanium endpoint agents directly).
2026-06-23.rapid7_query_hint: N/A (Primarily mobile device focused, not typically covered by InsightVM/IDR agents directly, may require mobile security solutions integration).

2026-06-23.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
2026-06-23.tldr: A credential harvesting operation, named FortiBleed, targeted FortiGate Firewalls, leading to the potential compromise of 110 million credentials. This indicates a significant data leak or unauthorized access, leveraging vulnerabilities to steal sensitive user data.
2026-06-23.action: Investigate FortiGate firewall logs for suspicious activity, credential access attempts, and ensure all FortiGate devices are patched and securely configured. Rotate credentials for users potentially impacted by this large-scale credential harvesting. Monitor network traffic for unusual outbound connections.

2026-06-23.title: Tata Electronics confirms cyberattack as hackers leak data
2026-06-23.tldr: Tata Electronics has officially confirmed that it was the target of a cyberattack that impacted parts of its IT infrastructure. As a result of this attack, hackers have leaked data, confirming a successful data exfiltration incident impacting the company's information.
2026-06-23.action: Investigate the full scope of the data leak, identify all affected individuals and data types, and comply with all relevant data breach notification laws. Implement enhanced monitoring across the IT infrastructure and review incident response procedures to prevent future occurrences.

2026-06-23.title: Healthtech firm Xolis suffers data breach impacting 1.4 million people
2026-06-23.tldr: Healthcare technology company Xsolis has reported a data breach that compromised sensitive data belonging to nearly 1.4 million individuals. The breach stemmed from a phishing attack that granted attackers unauthorized access to its network, leading to the exposure of personal health information.
2026-06-23.action: Assess the full extent of compromised data, notify all affected individuals, and provide credit monitoring or identity theft protection as appropriate. Enhance phishing training for all employees and implement stronger email security controls, including multi-factor authentication and advanced anti-phishing solutions.

2026-06-23.title: Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
2026-06-23.tldr: A high-severity Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20230, in Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) Server is now being actively exploited in the wild. This flaw allows attackers to make unauthorized requests on behalf of the vulnerable server, potentially leading to information disclosure or further internal network access.
2026-06-23.action: Immediately apply all available patches and security updates for Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server to address CVE-2026-20230. Isolate affected systems, review logs for any suspicious outgoing connections or unusual internal network activity, and implement network segmentation to limit potential lateral movement by attackers.
2026-06-23.affected_versions: Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server (Unified CM) versions affected by CVE-2026-20230. Refer to Cisco's official advisory for exact vulnerable versions and patch information.
2026-06-23.how_to_determine_impact: Check Cisco Unified CM installations for vulnerability by verifying the installed version against Cisco's security advisory for CVE-2026-20230. Additionally, look for unusual network connections originating from the Unified CM server, especially to internal systems or external unauthorized destinations, which could indicate exploitation.
2026-06-23.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications[Name and Version] from all machines. Filter for 'Cisco Unified Communications Manager'. Cross-reference detected versions with the Cisco advisory for CVE-2026-20230. Further, monitor network connections from CM servers for anomalous traffic patterns.
2026-06-23.rapid7_query_hint: Scan assets for `vulnerability.id = 'CVE-2026-20230'` or `installed_software.name CONTAINS 'Cisco Unified Communications Manager'` and filter by affected versions as per Cisco's advisory. Use InsightIDR to monitor network traffic for suspicious connections originating from these devices.

2026-06-23.title: FFmpeg PixelSmash Flaw Allows RCE on Video Players, Media Servers, NAS Appliances
2026-06-23.tldr: A newly identified vulnerability, dubbed 'PixelSmash,' in the FFmpeg multimedia framework allows for Remote Code Execution (RCE). This critical flaw affects a wide range of devices, including video players, media servers, and Network Attached Storage (NAS) appliances that process video files, enabling attackers to execute arbitrary code.
2026-06-23.action: Immediately update all systems, applications, and devices that utilize FFmpeg to the latest patched version available. Monitor systems processing media for any unusual activity or unauthorized code execution. Consider isolating media processing environments to minimize potential attack surface.
2026-06-23.affected_versions: FFmpeg versions affected by the 'PixelSmash' flaw. All applications, operating systems, and hardware devices (e.g., video players, media servers, NAS) that bundle or utilize vulnerable FFmpeg libraries are at risk. Consult FFmpeg project advisories for specific version ranges.
2026-06-23.how_to_determine_impact: Identify all applications and systems within your environment that utilize FFmpeg for multimedia processing. Check the version of the FFmpeg library being used (e.g., `ffmpeg -version`) and compare it against the official FFmpeg security advisories to determine vulnerability.
2026-06-23.tanium_query_hint: Use 'Get Installed Applications[Name and Version]' from all machines to identify instances of 'FFmpeg'. Additionally, 'Get Running Processes[Name, CommandLine]' can help identify applications that may be leveraging FFmpeg libraries. Cross-reference detected versions with known vulnerable ranges.
2026-06-23.rapid7_query_hint: Scan assets for software using FFmpeg libraries. Search `installed_software.name CONTAINS 'FFmpeg'` and verify detected versions against known vulnerable ranges. Prioritize devices identified as media servers, video players, or NAS appliances.

2026-06-23.title: New macOS ClickFix attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer
2026-06-23.tldr: A novel macOS attack campaign, named 'ClickFix,' is actively exploiting Terminal commands to silently download, mount, and launch malicious Disk Image (DMG) files. This technique allows the attackers to deploy infostealing malware without overt user interaction, posing a significant threat to macOS users.
2026-06-23.action: Implement robust Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) solutions on all macOS devices to detect and block unusual Terminal command executions or silent DMG mounting activities. Educate users on the dangers of unsolicited files and enforce strict application whitelisting policies to prevent unauthorized software execution.
2026-06-23.affected_versions: macOS devices targeted by the ClickFix campaign that can be coerced into silently mounting malicious DMGs. Specific macOS versions or configurations that are more vulnerable are not explicitly detailed; however, users of any macOS version should be vigilant.
2026-06-23.how_to_determine_impact: Monitor macOS endpoint logs for unexpected `hdiutil` (disk image utility) commands, particularly those executed silently or initiated by suspicious processes. Check for the presence of known infostealer malware families or unusual files in user download directories or temporary folders.
2026-06-23.tanium_query_hint: Use 'Get Running Processes[Name, CommandLine]' from all macOS machines to identify suspicious `hdiutil` commands or other unexpected process activity. Additionally, use 'Get File Exists[path]' for known infostealer file paths or indicators of compromise.
2026-06-23.rapid7_query_hint: Utilize InsightAgent queries on macOS assets to detect unusual process execution patterns or file modifications related to DMG mounting and infostealer installation. Look for `process.command_line CONTAINS 'hdiutil attach'` without corresponding user interaction logs.

2026-06-23.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
2026-06-23.tldr: A new executive order issued by President Trump establishes a mandatory deadline of 2030 for federal agencies to migrate their systems and data to post-quantum cryptography. This directive underscores the U.S. government's commitment to preparing for future cryptographic threats posed by quantum computing capabilities.
2026-06-23.action: Organizations, particularly those engaging with federal contracts or handling sensitive government data, should begin assessing their current cryptographic infrastructure. Develop a strategic roadmap for transitioning to post-quantum secure algorithms and technologies to meet anticipated regulatory and compliance requirements by 2030.

2026-06-24.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
2026-06-24.tldr: The "FortiBleed" operation exploited vulnerabilities in FortiGate firewalls to harvest an estimated 110 million credentials. This indicates a large-scale credential theft campaign targeting network devices.
2026-06-24.action: Immediately patch all FortiGate firewall devices to the latest secure versions. Conduct a thorough audit of all user accounts and enforce password resets and multi-factor authentication for all users, especially those with access to FortiGate devices.

2026-06-24.title: BeyondTrust, LastPass Impacted by Klue-Salesforce Incident
2026-06-24.tldr: BeyondTrust and LastPass were impacted by an incident involving third-party vendor Klue and Salesforce. While specific details on the nature of the impact are not fully clear, it highlights supply chain risks.
2026-06-24.action: Review all third-party vendor access and data sharing agreements. Implement strict access controls, principle of least privilege, and regular security audits for all vendors, especially those handling sensitive data or providing critical services.

2026-06-24.title: Malicious Edge extension abuses Native Messaging as bridge to malware
2026-06-24.tldr: A malicious Microsoft Edge extension, "Edgecution," was used in a ransomware attack. It exploited Native Messaging to escape the browser sandbox and deploy a Python-based backdoor, leading to a ransomware payload.
2026-06-24.action: Audit all installed browser extensions for legitimacy. Implement strict browser extension policies and regularly review endpoint security logs for signs of unauthorized processes or backdoor activity. Block "Edgecution" extension.

2026-06-24.title: Mandiant reveals how Cisco SD-WAN zero-day attacks gained root access
2026-06-24.tldr: Mandiant has detailed how hackers exploited a zero-day vulnerability, CVE-2026-20245, in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN. This attack allowed the creation of rogue root accounts on targeted devices, providing full control to attackers.
2026-06-24.action: Immediately apply security updates from Cisco for Catalyst SD-WAN to address CVE-2026-20245. Review all user accounts on Cisco SD-WAN devices for any unauthorized or rogue root accounts and revoke them. Monitor for suspicious activity on these devices.
2026-06-24.affected_versions: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN (All versions prior to the patch for CVE-2026-20245)
2026-06-24.how_to_determine_impact: Check Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN devices for installed software versions. Refer to Cisco's official advisory for CVE-2026-20245 to identify specific vulnerable versions and indicators of compromise, such as rogue root accounts.
2026-06-24.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications with Name 'Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN' and check version. Use Get User Accounts to look for suspicious or newly created root-level accounts on affected systems.
2026-06-24.rapid7_query_hint: Search for assets with "Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN". Filter vulnerabilities by "CVE-2026-20245". Use Insight Agent queries to check for specific file hashes or user account anomalies identified by Cisco's advisory.

2026-06-24.title: Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
2026-06-24.tldr: A high-severity Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability, CVE-2026-20230, in Cisco Unified Communications Manager (CM) Server is actively being exploited in attacks. This flaw could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to perform various actions depending on the target server.
2026-06-24.action: Apply all available security patches from Cisco for Unified Communications Manager to address CVE-2026-20230. Review network logs for unusual connection attempts to Cisco CM servers and look for indicators of compromise related to SSRF exploitation.
2026-06-24.affected_versions: Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server (All versions prior to the patch for CVE-2026-20230)
2026-06-24.how_to_determine_impact: Identify all Cisco Unified CM Servers. Check the installed version against Cisco's official security advisory for CVE-2026-20230 to confirm vulnerability. Monitor network activity for unusual HTTP requests or connections originating from CM servers.
2026-06-24.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications with Name 'Cisco Unified Communications Manager' and check version. Use Get Open Ports and Get Running Processes to identify instances and potential anomalous activity.
2026-06-24.rapid7_query_hint: Search for assets with "Cisco Unified Communications Manager". Filter vulnerabilities by "CVE-2026-20230". Review network logs for indicators of SSRF exploitation.

2026-06-24.title: CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
2026-06-24.tldr: CISA has issued a warning regarding multiple maximum severity vulnerabilities in Ubiquiti UniFi OS that are being actively exploited in attacks. These flaws could lead to full compromise of affected devices. The warning also reiterates active exploitation of Lantronix serial-to-ethernet servers.
2026-06-24.action: Immediately identify all Ubiquiti UniFi OS devices within your network and apply the latest security patches. Ensure that these devices are not exposed directly to the internet if possible, or are behind robust firewall rules. Conduct thorough security audits on affected systems.
2026-06-24.affected_versions: Ubiquiti UniFi OS (All versions prior to the latest security updates addressing these maximum severity flaws)
2026-06-24.how_to_determine_impact: Identify all Ubiquiti UniFi OS devices. Check the installed firmware/software versions against official Ubiquiti security advisories for maximum severity flaws. Look for signs of compromise as detailed in vendor alerts.
2026-06-24.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications with Name 'Ubiquiti UniFi OS' and check version. Use Get Network Adapter Configuration to identify Ubiquiti devices.
2026-06-24.rapid7_query_hint: Search for assets with "Ubiquiti UniFi OS". Scan these devices for known vulnerabilities. Review network logs and system logs for any unauthorized access or unusual activity on these devices.
